items.age_note: Several members' most recent statements flip from concern to cautious optimism after the Nov 2024 election; the split is partly temporal.

risk_regime.rationale: Seven of nine members express a risk_on posture (convictions ~65–86) with no risk_off dissent; the remaining two (the cryptography/mechanism-design researchers) explicitly hold no market view, so they neither add nor subtract from the directional signal. The consensus is a structural, builder-oriented risk-on: bullish on fundamental infrastructure and adoption (ZK, stablecoins, L2s, interop, AI×crypto rails) and reinforced by late-2024 post-election regulatory optimism — but explicitly risk-OFF on speculation, the 'casino,' and memecoins. Conviction is set below the bullish bloc's raw mean to reflect that two members abstain and that the optimism is conditioned on building/adoption, not speculative price action.

notes: Consensus weights each item by BOTH coverage and conviction, and damps conviction where members are split. Clear high-coverage, high-conviction signals: bullish ZK/zkVM and stablecoins; bearish memecoins and speculation. Genuinely split items were neutralized or damped: restaking (cov 3, σ=0.94 — Yahya/Wuollet bullish on it as 'verifiable cloud'/security primitive while Roughgarden flags combinatorial cascading-attack risk; net neutral, omitted from the lists rather than forced to a side), on-chain-adoption / dao-legal-entities / recursive-snarks (all σ=1.0, fully split → dropped as no-consensus), and crypto-policy-regulation / content-authenticity / ai-crypto (high dispersion → logged at reduced conviction with the split called out in-line). Lone but very-high-conviction views were deliberately faded out of the consensus lists per the weighting rule rather than allowed to dominate — notably Jennings' control-based-decentralization (98) and DUNA/DAO legal wrappers, Roughgarden's consensus-protocol-design (95) / TFM / MEV / restaking-risk research, Thaler's sumcheck/lookup/Binius proof-system program (85–95), and Wuollet's decentralized-energy (92). These represent specialist depth from single members, not house-wide conviction, though several (the proof-system work especially) feed the broad ZK consensus that IS top-ranked. Single-coverage adjacent bullish themes worth monitoring but below the inclusion bar: payments, agentic-payments, blockspace-demand, decentralized-internet-infra, light-clients, prediction-markets, rwa, desci, governance-dao, wallet-infrastructure, modular-da. The desk skews toward infrastructure/protocol theses over tradeable token calls, so token lists are intentionally short and ETH/BTC engagement is largely structural rather than price-directional.
